title: Align .rules and init-script rules template for mandatory ticket status persistence
summary: Update the checked-in `.rules` file and the `.rules` template embedded in `zed-agentops-init.sh` so both explicitly require ticket status persistence and synchronization across per-ticket JSON files, `tickets_list.json`, and runtime execution flow. The goal is to remove ambiguity about whether status updates are optional and to keep newly initialized projects aligned with the repository contract.
outputs: Updated checked-in `.rules` text for mandatory ticket status persistence
Updated `zed-agentops-init.sh` embedded `.rules` template with matching requirements
Regression or contract checks that lock script/rules alignment for ticket status persistence
acceptance_criteria: The checked-in `.rules` file explicitly states that ticket status updates must be persisted to both per-ticket JSON files and `tickets_list.json`.
The checked-in `.rules` file explicitly states that ticket status persistence is mandatory during execution, not optional bookkeeping.
The `zed-agentops-init.sh` embedded `.rules` template matches the checked-in `.rules` content for ticket status persistence and synchronization requirements.
The rules text makes clear when ticket status persistence must occur within the work loop.
Tests or contract checks verify that the checked-in `.rules` file and init-script template remain aligned on the ticket status persistence requirements.
tasks: Review the checked-in `.rules` file and the `zed-agentops-init.sh` embedded `.rules` template for wording gaps around ticket status persistence.
Update `.rules` so it explicitly requires persistence of ticket status changes to both per-ticket JSON files and `tickets_list.json`.
Clarify in `.rules` when those status updates must happen during the canonical work loop.
Update the `zed-agentops-init.sh` heredoc template so newly initialized projects receive the same mandatory ticket status persistence contract.
Add or update tests that lock the alignment between the checked-in `.rules` file and the init-script template.
notes: This ticket focuses on rules and scaffold alignment, not on changing the ticket status enum.
Do not introduce a separate or competing status model for docs versus runtime.
Keep the checked-in `.rules` file and the init-script template text synchronized closely enough that future changes can be validated by tests.
